case_metadata.source: NIST CFReDS (Computer Forensic Reference Data Sets)
case_metadata.subject: Greg Schardt aka 'Mr. Evil' — wireless traffic interception case
case_metadata.ground_truth_provenance: NIST official TestAnswers.pdf — community-verified, peer-reviewed reference dataset

ground_truth_findings.claim: Registered owner of system is 'Greg Schardt'
ground_truth_findings.expected_dart_mcp_function: (registry hive parsing — partially covered by sift_amcacheparser_parse for AmCache; full SOFTWARE hive parsing is roadmap)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: dart-mcp Phase 1 has read-only AmCache + SHIMCache + ShellBags adapters but not generic SOFTWARE hive value extraction. Phase 2 deliverable.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Account 'Mr. Evil' is the primary user account
ground_truth_findings.expected_dart_mcp_function: analyze_windows_logons (SAM-derived account enumeration is adjacent capability)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Current analyze_windows_logons consumes pre-extracted Security event JSON. Native SAM hive parsing not yet covered. Phase 2.
AmCache records every executed binary with InstallDate + path. Prefetch confirms execution. dart-mcp v0.5.3 has both adapters. Secondary evidence: Prefetch/*.pf.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Two network cards: Xircom CardBus + Compaq WL110 Wireless
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Same as F-CFR-001 — generic SOFTWARE hive value extraction is roadmap.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Ethereal capture file 'Interception' in My Documents — intercepted wireless traffic
ground_truth_findings.expected_dart_mcp_function: parse_browser_history is adjacent (Documents folder enumeration); detect_exfiltration could flag if filesize unusual
ground_truth_findings.rationale: MFT walk + size anomaly possible, but specific filename detection requires file-content YARA rule. Phase 2 dart-synth can synthesize this rule from known evidence. Secondary evidence: Settings/Mr. Evil/My Documents/Interception.

Browser history extraction works for Chrome/Edge schemas; IE6/Outlook Express on XP uses different schema (index.dat) — Phase 2. Secondary evidence: Settings/Mr. Evil/Local Settings/Application Data/Identities/{...}/Microsoft/Outlook Express.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Last user to logon was 'Mr. Evil' (logged on 15 times total)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Function works on Security event JSON. CFReDS has live registry — extraction step needed. Phase 1 can verify if pre-extracted. Secondary evidence: Security event log.
Recycle bin INFO2/$I$R parsing is candidate Phase 2 primitive.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Anti-virus check positive — viruses present on disk
ground_truth_findings.evidence_path: Multiple file paths — community AV scan result
ground_truth_findings.expected_dart_mcp_function: (YARA rule scan — Phase 2 with sift_yara adapter)
ground_truth_findings.rationale: YARA adapter exists but no rules bundled; needs Sigma/YARA rule library.
Generic SYSTEM hive key reading is roadmap.

directly_detectable_findings_summary.explanation: This is a deliberately honest disclosure. CFReDS Hacking Case is a 2004 Windows XP image with content-centric ground truth (what was inside specific files). dart-mcp v0.5.3 is artifact-centric (timeline, persistence mechanisms, lateral movement signals). The two paradigms partially overlap. The recall=0.10 here is NOT a bug — it correctly indicates that 9 of 10 sampled findings need either (a) generic registry hive value extraction (Phase 2 #45) or (b) file content YARA scanning (Phase 2 #10) which are tracked roadmap items, not currently shipped. Reference variant recall=1.0 measures dart-mcp's ability to detect IOCs it CLAIMS to detect; CFReDS recall measures expansion potential.

gaps_identified_by_cfreds_integration.description: Generic SOFTWARE/SYSTEM/SAM hive value extraction (not just AmCache)
IE6/Outlook Express index.dat parser (legacy browser artifacts)
YARA rule library + scan primitive (sift_yara adapter exists; rules don't)

phase_2_gap_analysis.value_of_cfreds_integration: CFReDS gave us a community-verified roadmap for what dart-mcp's NEXT 4 primitives must be. Without this benchmark, we'd ship Phase 2 prioritizing developer intuition; with it, we ship Phase 2 prioritizing measured gaps.
